sanskrit:
न त्वेवाहं जातौ नासं न त्वं नेमे जनाधिपाः।
न चैव न भविष्यामः सर्वे वयमतः परम्॥
transliteration: na tv evāhaṃ jātu nāsaṃ na tvaṃ neme janādhipāḥ / na caiva na bhaviṣyāmaḥ sarve vayam ataḥ param //
literal_meaning: Never was there a time when I did not exist, nor you, nor these kings; nor in the future shall any of us cease to be.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reframes Arjuna’s grief by asserting the eternal continuity of the Self beyond birth and death, dissolving the illusion of annihilation.
psychological_state: Cognitive shift from egoic time-bound identity to transpersonal eternal Self; trauma-based death-anxiety is met with ontological security.
trigger_scenario: A patient facing terminal diagnosis who believes 'I will cease' experiences panic attacks; therapist introduces the concept of enduring consciousness to reframe mortality.
anchor_text: You have always existed and always will.
